title: The David Fincher Rankings, Part II
format: Sean Fennessey, Amanda Dobbins, and Chris Ryan close out the David Fincher countdown, then Sean talks to Miranda July about 'Kajillionaire.'

films.title: Black Widow
films.note: Pushed off 2020 along with most of Disney's slate; Amanda calls the loss of the year's big movies a real one.

films.title: Death on the Nile
films.note: Amanda's most-anticipated casualty of the delays: 'a personal loss.'

films.title: Tenet
films.note: Its underwhelming opening is why everyone else is punting, per Amanda: the industry's decisions come down to money, not safety.

films.title: Mulan
films.note: Its Disney+ premium numbers apparently weren't strong enough to push more titles onto the platform.

films.title: West Side Story
films.note: One of the biggest presumed 2020 Oscar contenders, now bumped a full year to December 2021.

films.title: The Trial of the Chicago 7
films.note: Sean and Amanda saw it the night before taping; Sorkin's script has Sean already hyped to discuss it at length.

segments.heading: The Unmade Fincher Files
segments.note: on Alan Siegel's Ringer piece cataloging the projects that got away

films.title: Se7en
films.note: Cited as the model for how Fincher elevates a premise that could've been corny or predictable, same as the Zodiac idea.

films.title: Mission: Impossible III
films.note: Fincher was attached before J.J. Abrams came aboard; both hosts think a Fincher version, with Ethan Hunt getting the dark-cynic treatment, would've been fascinating.

films.title: Burnt
films.note: The Bradley Cooper film that effectively absorbed Fincher's shelved Anthony Bourdain adaptation, 'Seared'; Chris jokingly calls it 'one of the great plane movies.'

films.title: Steve Jobs
films.note: Chris says the Danny Boyle version is underrated, though he still wants to know what Fincher's take would've looked like.

films.title: The Dirt
films.note: The Mötley Crüe biopic was originally a Fincher project before landing at Netflix; Chris calls the version that got made 'not successful at all.'

films.title: Black Dahlia
films.note: Chris's biggest what-if: Brian De Palma's adaptation of James Ellroy's book is 'a very stiff movie' despite De Palma being a clear Fincher influence, and Chris sees echoes of the unmade Fincher version in both Mindhunter and Zodiac.

segments.heading: The Top 10 David Fincher Things, Concluded
segments.note: the countdown finishes here; three music videos and Mindhunter also charted in the top 10 but are cut from this list as non-films
segments.subject: David Fincher

films.title: Zodiac
films.note: No. 1. Sean's pick, seconded by Chris: 'in the 100 greatest films of all time conversation.' Amanda concedes the point despite coming in ready to argue for Social Network.

films.title: The Social Network
films.note: No. 2. All three agreed it's the best movie of the 2010s; Sean calls it a movie about the world where Zodiac is a movie about life.

films.title: Fight Club
films.note: No. 3. Sean's cooled on it over time, calling it more polemic than movie now; Amanda finds it gimmicky; Chris still ranks it third.

films.title: Gone Girl
films.note: No. 4. Sean likes it but thinks the movie loses its grip on Rosamund Pike's character; all three note the comically fast Dateline-style cuts.

films.title: Se7en
films.note: No. 5. Chris and Amanda both put it here despite loving it, essentially conceding it can't crack the top four against this group.

films.title: The Curious Case of Benjamin Button
films.note: Landed at No. 8 on air, behind the excluded music videos and Mindhunter. Amanda's defense: dismissing it outright means 'not curious enough' about how wide Fincher's range is; she also flags it as the breakout for Mahershala Ali and Taraji P. Henson.

films.title: Kajillionaire
films.note: July on the idea's origin (a half-awake vision of a family, dictated into her phone), the total creative control Plan B and Annapurna gave her, and casting Evan Rachel Wood and Debra Winger.

referenced.group: In passing, during the unmade-projects talk
films: The Girl with the Dragon Tattoo
Me and You and Everyone We Know
